item.title: Gilead Sciences
item.description:
• Summary: Gilead Sciences featured advancements in HIV treatment and a reinforced focus on oncology. The company highlighted progress in its HIV pipeline, which may involve long-acting regimens or novel agents like the integrase inhibitor bictegravir. Gilead also emphasized its strategic commitment to expanding its oncology research and development efforts.
  • MoAs: Bictegravir: integrase strand transfer inhibitor (INSTI).
  • Sources: STAT News, STAT News

item.title: GlaxoSmithKline
item.description:
• Summary: Coverage of GlaxoSmithKline (GSK) focuses on two key therapeutic areas. One report discusses the performance and pipeline of GSK's respiratory disease portfolio, which includes drugs for asthma and COPD. The second article highlights the company's work in HIV prevention, including the long-acting injectable regimen Cabenuva (cabotegravir + rilpivirine) and the investigational agent cabotegravir for pre-exposure prophylaxis (PrEP).
  • MoAs: Cabotegravir (in Cabenuva or for PrEP): Integrase strand transfer inhibitor (INSTI). Rilpivirine (in Cabenuva): Non-nucleoside reverse transcriptase inhibitor (NNRTI).
  • Sources: STAT News, STAT News

item.title: Vaccines
item.description:
• Summary: The mRNA vaccine platform, exemplified by Pfizer-BioNTech's BNT162b2 and Moderna's mRNA-1273, has revolutionized rapid vaccine development, particularly for COVID-19. Global access issues highlight disparities in distribution and equity.
  • MoAs: BNT162b2 and mRNA-1273 use lipid nanoparticles to deliver mRNA encoding viral antigens, eliciting an immune response.
  • Sources: Stat News, Stat News

item.title: Antibiotics
item.description:
• Summary: Antibiotic resistance is a growing crisis due to overuse and a stagnant pipeline. Economic and regulatory challenges hinder new antibiotic development, threatening public health.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: Stat News, Stat News

item.title: Infectious Diseases
item.description:
• Summary: This theme covers the ongoing threat of emerging infectious diseases and the development of novel antiviral therapeutics. It discusses surveillance for new pathogens, pandemic potential, and the pipeline for antiviral drugs targeting a range of viral families.
  • MoAs: Antiviral drugs encompass various mechanisms of action including viral polymerase inhibitors, protease inhibitors, entry inhibitors, and agents that modulate host immune responses.
  • Sources: STAT News, STAT News

item.title: Antimicrobial Resistance
item.description:
• Summary: This theme addresses the critical public health threat of antimicrobial resistance (AMR). It covers global surveillance efforts to track resistant pathogens and the urgent need for innovative antibacterial drugs, highlighting the challenges in the R&D pipeline for new antibiotics.
  • MoAs: New antibacterial drugs target a variety of mechanisms, including novel cell wall synthesis inhibitors, protein synthesis inhibitors, and agents disrupting bacterial membrane integrity or metabolic pathways.
  • Sources: STAT News, STAT News
